_note: The enablement spine: tracks → areas → enablers — the SKELETON of the second axis (ids, names, order, hue, tagline). Same rules as lifecycle.json: editorial PROSE (canon + the editor's note) lives in content/tracks/<id>.md, keyed by enabler id for leaf-level lines; edit either, then run build-content to recompile + validate. ids are stable references: enabling use cases place by track + enabler id, and lifecycle use cases may cite enabler ids in enabled_by — keep them stable when renaming. UNLIKE the lifecycle, this taxonomy is EMERGENT by design: tracks are currently FLAT (no areas tier) and each compiles as one implicit area; introduce "areas" arrays (the surfaces tier) only when enablers actually cluster — it's a data edit, not a redesign. Everything here is provisional: no endorsements on this spine yet (locked decision D5).

tracks.name: Data owners
tracks.tagline: Make your data legible to agents you'll never meet.

tracks.tagline: Run the platform as a product whose newest user is an agent.

tracks.tagline: Make direction something an agent can cite, not guess.
